elements.sent-2411-001: Let $S$ be a fixed set of primes and let $(X_{l )_{l\geq 1}$ be the $X$-coordinates of the positive integer solutions $(X, Y)$ of the Pell equation $X^2-dY^2 = 1$ corresponding to a non-square integer $d>1$.}
elements.sent-2411-002: We show that there are only a finite number of non-square integers $d>1$ such that there are at least two different elements of the sequence $(X_{l )_{l\geq 1}$ that can be represented as a sum of $S$-units with a fixed number of terms.}

elements.sent-2411-004: The problem of finding specific terms of a linear recurrence sequence of some particular form has a very rich history.
elements.sent-2411-005: Peth\H{o \cite{Petho1982 and Shorey-Stewart \cite{Shorey1983 independently studied the perfect powers in a non-degenerate binary recurrence sequence.}}}

elements.sent-2411-011: Recently, Bert\'{o k et al., \cite{bhpr2 under some mild assumptions gave finiteness result for the solutions of the Diophantine equation \begin{equation}\label{eq0b} U_{n} = b_1 p_1^{z_1} + \cdots+ b_s p_s^{z_s}\end{equation} in non-negative integers $z_1, \ldots, z_s$ and $n$, where $(U_{n )_{n\geq 0}$ is a binary non-degenerate recurrence sequence of positive discriminant, $p_{1 , \ldots, p_{s}$ are given primes and $b_1, \ldots, b_s$ are fixed integers.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: Diophantine equations combining both $S$-units and recurrence sequences has been well studied by many authors (see \cite{bhpr1, bhpr2, EGL2020, hs ).}
elements.sent-2411-013: Suppose $S$ is the set of distinct primes $p_1,\ldots,p_{l}$.
elements.sent-2411-014: Then a rational integer $z$ is an $S$-unit if $z$ can be written as \begin{equation}\label{eq0} z = \pm p_{1}^{e_1}\cdots p_{l}^{e_{l}},\end{equation} where $e_1, \ldots, e_{l}$ are non-negative integers and we denote the set of $S$-units by $\mathcal{U _S$.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Let $(U_n)_{n\geq 0}$ be a recurrence sequence of order $k$ with $k\geq 2$.
elements.sent-2411-016: B\'erczes et al. \cite{bhpr1 considered the equation \begin{equation}\label{eq0sunit} U_n = z_1+\cdots+z_r,\end{equation} with some arbitrary but fixed $r\geq 1$, in unknown $n\geq 0$ and $z_1, \ldots, z_r\in \mathcal{U _S$ and establish finiteness results for the solutions of \eqref{eq0sunit .}}}

elements.sent-2411-021: What can one say about the number of solutions of the containment $X_m\in \mathbb{U}$ for a generic $d$? elements.sent-2411-022: Erazo et al., \cite{EGL2020 showed under certain conditions that there are only a finite number non-square integers $d>1$ such that there are at least two different elements of the sequence $(X_m)_{m\geq 1}$ that can be represented as a linear combination of prime powers with fixed primes, i.e., \begin{equation}X_m = c_1p_1^{n_1}+\cdots + c_sp_s^{n_s}.\end{equation}.}
